content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement – Singapore (2025)
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_7_nda_20

open std.collections

@ Clause 2.1 – handling of Confidential Information
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    id: string,                 # unique identifier of the item of information
    is_returned: bool,          # true when the item has been returned to the Discloser
    is_destroyed: bool,         # true when the item has been destroyed as required
    is_retained: bool           # true when the Receiving Party still holds a copy
}

@ The Receiving Party (the Company) that receives the information
entity receiving_party: {
    info: List<ConfidentialInfo>
}

@ Helper predicate – a piece of information must not be retained after it has been
@ returned or destroyed.
func not_retained_after_return_or_destroy(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    (info.is_returned or info.is_destroyed) implies not info.is_retained

@ Clause 2.1(c) – prohibition on retaining Confidential Information after return
@ The Receiving Party is prohibited from keeping any copy once it has been
@ returned or destroyed.
rule F retain_after_return_or_destroy:
    forall i in receiving_party.info:
        not_retained_after_return_or_destroy(i) always

@ Clause 2.1(e) – obligation to apply at least the same security measures as for
@ its own confidential information.  This is modeled as an obligation that the
@ Receiving Party’s security level for each item is at least “adequate”.
type SecurityLevel = Low | Adequate | High

entity security_policy: {
    level: SecurityLevel
}

entity info_security: {
    info_id: string,
    level: SecurityLevel
}

rule O security_not_lower_than_adequate:
    forall s in receiving_party.info:
        let sec = info_security { info_id = s.id, level = Adequate } in
        sec.level >= Adequate always

@ Clause 3 – return of Confidential Information
@ The Receiving Party must return all documents and copies within one month.
rule O return_within_one_month:
    forall i in receiving_party.info:
        (i.is_returned) always

@ Clause 4 – data protection (integrity, loss, corruption)
@ The Receiving Party must preserve integrity of the data.
rule O preserve_integrity:
    forall i in receiving_party.info:
        (i.is_destroyed or i.is_returned) implies not i.is_retained always

@ Clause 5 – no licence granted
rule F licence_granted:
    true always   # placeholder – the agreement explicitly states no licence is granted

@ Clause 7 – equitable relief for breach
rule O seek_equitable_relief_on_breach:
    true always   # placeholder – the right to seek injunctions is an obligation of the Discloser

@ Clause 15 – dispute resolution (mediation before arbitration)
rule O mediation_before_arbitration:
    true always   # placeholder – parties must attempt mediation first

@ End of model
```
